Title: Maize-Tripsacum-Teosinte allopolyploid (MTP), a novel dwarf mutant inducer tool in maize

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73 coding sequences tested in heterologous interaction assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with ZmCYP90D1 and likely supports its electron transfer/hydroxylation activity in BR biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmFDX2 was identified as a ZmCYP90D1 interactor by Y2H. 2. Interaction was validated by BiFC and split luciferase assays. 3. The study infers that ZmFDX2 helps support ZmCYP90D1 function in BR synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; split firefly luciferase complementation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane system/ER-associated interaction signal in tobacco cells
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive cofactor partner rather than mapped causal dwarf gene
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant height and internode elongation through BR pathway support

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73 coding sequences tested in heterologous interaction assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with ZmCYP90D1 and likely supports its electron transfer/hydroxylation activity in BR biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmFDX5 was identified as a ZmCYP90D1 interactor by Y2H. 2. Interaction was validated by BiFC and split luciferase assays. 3. The study infers that ZmFDX5 helps support ZmCYP90D1 function in BR synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; split firefly luciferase complementation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane system/ER-associated interaction signal in tobacco cells
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive cofactor partner rather than mapped causal dwarf gene
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant height and internode elongation through BR pathway support

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73 coding sequence tested in heterologous interaction assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with ZmCYP90D1 and likely provides reductant/electron-transfer support required for CYP90D1-mediated BR biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNAD(P)H was identified as a ZmCYP90D1 interactor by Y2H. 2. Interaction was validated by BiFC and split luciferase assays. 3. The study infers that ZmNAD(P)H supports ZmCYP90D1 catalytic function in BR synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; split firefly luciferase complementation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane system/ER-associated interaction signal in tobacco cells
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed as interactor supporting P450 function; exact molecular identity beyond reported name not clarified in the text
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports BR-mediated growth and internode elongation

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 allele; Tripsacum parent TZ07-derived indel-4 allele in d024
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: d024, H024, B73, SW1611; improved hybrids B73d024 × Mo17d024
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in examined tissues; high expression in ear internodes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in stems/ear nodes of d024 and CRISPR knockout lines than WT; generally high in ear internodes at elongation stage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Tripsacum-derived promoter indel-4 allele can be introgressed by marker-assisted selection to create semi-dwarf, thicker-stem maize suited for high-density planting while maintaining grain yield and improving harvest index
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Responsive to exogenous epiBL/BR; d024 plant height increases with higher epiBL concentrations; dark-grown seedlings show reduced hypocotyl elongation consistent with BR deficiency; evaluated under high planting density field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not definitively established as rate-limiting; supported as an important late-stage BR biosynthetic control point downstream in the pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate/late precursors for bioactive brassinosteroids, promoting BR accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fine mapping identified d024 as Zm00001d039453/zmCYP90D1. 2. A 191-bp promoter deletion (indel-4) from Tripsacum lowers promoter activity and expression, causing semi-dwarfism. 3. CRISPR knockout in maize phenocopied/severely enhanced dwarfism, while overexpression in Arabidopsis increased plant height. 4. d024 and knockout plants had reduced castasterone and typhasterol, indicating BR deficiency. 5. Introgression of the indel-4 allele into elite hybrids maintained yield under high density and improved harvest index.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates brassinosteroid biosynthesis and stem internode elongation; reduced expression or knockout decreases castasterone/typhasterol and causes semi-dwarf to severe dwarf plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced straw biomass and slight decreases in some organ traits/seed size in dwarf materials; overly strong loss-of-function can cause more severe dwarf defects
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Brassinosteroid biosynthesis, likely campesterol-to-castasterone segment in the early C-6 oxidation pathway and corresponding 28-homo-brassinolide route
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Castasterone-related late BR intermediates; likely contributes to typhasterol/castasterone production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes one or more steroid hydroxylation steps from campesterol-derived intermediates toward castasterone in BR biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cell elongation, internode growth, hypocotyl elongation, meristem formation, and overall plant height architecture
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causative natural variation emphasized in this paper is promoter indel-4 deleting a BRRE motif; exon variants were present but not supported as causal. The applied effect was studied in maize, while the beneficial dwarfing allele originated from tetraploid Tripsacum dactyloides via MTP.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters in maize; 35S used for Arabidopsis overexpression and transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA-seq and fine mapping; promoter sequencing/activity assay; CRISPR knockout in maize; Arabidopsis overexpression and Arabidopsis homolog mutant analysis; BR metabolite profiling by GC-MS; hormone response assays

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73 promoter context; Tripsacum-indel promoter context tested in tobacco transient system
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via binding BRRE motif in target promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream activator of ZmCYP90D1 expression; loss of BRRE motif in the Tripsacum indel-4 weakens activation and lowers BR biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmBES1/BZR1-5 binds the BRRE motif in the ZmCYP90D1 promoter. 2. Binding/transactivation is stronger with the B73 promoter than with the Tripsacum promoter carrying the 191-bp deletion. 3. This reduced activation explains lower ZmCYP90D1 expression in d024 and contributes to BR-deficient semi-dwarfism.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Brassinosteroid biosynthesis through transcriptional control of a late-pathway enzyme gene
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BR signaling context; tested by Y1H, EMSA, and dual-luciferase in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ZmCYP90D1 transcription; weakened regulation caused by promoter deletion reduces BR biosynthesis and plant height
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports internode elongation and normal maize stature through BR production
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ZmCYP90D1 transcription via BRRE binding
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal causal lesion is in the target promoter, not in ZmBES1/BZR1-5 coding sequence
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an upstream regulatory lever for tuning BR biosynthesis, but this paper mainly supports promoter editing of ZmCYP90D1 BRRE region rather than direct manipulation of ZmBES1/BZR1-5
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; transient dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S effector in transient assay; ZmCYP90D1B73 and ZmCYP90D1Tripsacum native promoters as reporters
